route.name: Sattitla Flow
route.description: Official/public GPX loop on well-maintained gravel, mellow two-track, and paved connectors around Medicine Lake.
route.surfaceMix: Official/public GPX loop on well-maintained gravel, mellow two-track, and paved connectors around Medicine Lake.
route.bestSeason: Snow-free May/June to October; avoid the Pit River Tribe third-weekend July gathering window.

difficultyProfile.surface: Official/public GPX loop on well-maintained gravel, mellow two-track, and paved connectors around Medicine Lake.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Water at Medicine Lake and mile 9; no water roughly mile 9-40 unless caching near Jot Dean Ice Cave.
resources.food: No services on route; bring all meals.
resources.camping: Medicine Lake area campgrounds before/after; Paynes Spring, Blanche Lake, or Bullseye Lake on route need current confirmation.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before ride-ready promotion.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Clean 2-day candidate once water/camp status is refreshed.
Plan a water cache if needed and remove it after the ride.
Respect cultural guidance around Medicine Lake.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control ride-ready promotion.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: reject/rework for ride_ready; target window and GPX pass, but exact camp/open/water status and mile 9-40 water-cache/carry plan are unresolved
blockers: No services on route and roughly mile 9-40 water gap needs explicit plan.
USFS Sattitla Highlands information says the volcanic area is nearly devoid of surface water drainages.
Sattitla water plan is not ride-ready: official USFS source says the area is nearly devoid of surface water drainages.
Sattitla Flow remains conditional because Medicine Lake access/campground status, smoke/fire check, and remote no-transit bailout must be explicitly accepted.
Official USFS monument information says Sattitla is nearly devoid of surface water drainages.
Bikepacking source notes no water between approximately mile 9 and mile 40.
Medicine Lake campground/spigot and selected first-night camp water are not officially confirmed.
Modoc National Forest high fire danger and departure-week smoke/weather remain unresolved.
No reliable transit bailout is available; this is remote self-rescue only.
No source-backed exact July 10-13 campsite booking/open-status/water plan.
Mile 9-40 no-water gap lacks a confirmed legal cache/carry plan.
Shasta-Trinity and Klamath fire restrictions require exact route-jurisdiction compliance before promotion.
Smoke/AQI and weather cannot be cleared this far ahead.

Refresh Modoc, Shasta-Trinity, and Klamath alerts/fire restrictions plus AirNow/smoke and NWS forecast July 8-10.
openQuestions: What changed in the July 9-10 final refresh for closures, weather, smoke, water, and camps?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: 58.1 local-GPX miles over 2 ride days fits July 10-13, 2026 and avoids the third-weekend July cultural gathering no-ride window.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Local public/output GPX is present and internally usable with about 3047 trackpoints and 22 waypoints.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Modoc, Shasta-Trinity, and Klamath alerts/fire restrictions plus AirNow/smoke and NWS forecast must be refreshed July 8-10.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: Route crosses Modoc, Shasta-Trinity, and Klamath National Forest/monument lands; exact jurisdiction and fire-order compliance need mapping before promotion.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Route source says only mile 9 and first-night camp water exist, no water from mile 9 to mile 40, and suggests a Jot Dean Ice Cave-area water stash; no legal/retrievable cache plan or official spigot status is confirmed.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: Candidate camp plan exists, but official current facility/open/water status for a selected Medicine Lake and first-night camp is not confirmed.
promotionChecklist.mapArtifactsNote: Public GPX and terrain/topographic map artifacts are present.

days.name: Sattitla Flow segment 1
days.camp: Medicine Lake area campgrounds before/after; Paynes Spring, Blanche Lake, or Bullseye Lake on route need current confirmation.

waypoints.name: ALT Continue to add on Fourmile Tree Molds and Crater Glass Flow

logistics: USFS Sattitla Highlands National Monument page describes hiking, biking, camping, hunting, scenic driving, and canoeing as recreation opportunities. https://www.fs.usda.gov/visit/national-monuments/sattitla-highlands-national-monument

USFS Sattitla page says the area is nearly devoid of surface water drainages and precipitation filters into porous volcanic rock; treat water as a hard gate. https://www.fs.usda.gov/visit/national-monuments/sattitla-highlands-national-monument
Subagent found direct public GPX and terrain-map artifacts pass.
Conditional camp plan: Medicine Lake campgrounds for night-before/vehicle logistics; Paynes Spring, Blanche Lake, or Bullseye Lake free campground for ride night.
California-priority subagent confirmed public GPX and terrain-map artifacts.
A legal water cache may be needed, but it must be explicitly planned and removed; no cache should be implied by the app.

readinessEvidence.sourceName: USFS Sattitla Highlands National Monument page
readinessEvidence.finding: USFS lists biking and camping among recreation opportunities, but exact route roads/camps still need current forest/monument checks.

readinessEvidence.sourceName: USFS Sattitla Highlands National Monument page
readinessEvidence.finding: USFS describes the area as nearly devoid of surface water drainages; route water/cache plan is a hard promotion gate.

USFS says the monument includes exceptional outdoor recreation, including biking/camping language on the page and continued public access under the monument framework, but route-level road/trail legality still needs forest travel-management confirmation.

readinessEvidence.finding: USFS says Sattitla is nearly devoid of surface water drainages, so this cannot be ride-ready without a specific water carry/cache plan.
